questions.question: What is the primary purpose of `git stash`?
options: To save uncommitted changes to a temporary storage area and restore a clean working directory
To commit changes to a new branch automatically
questions.answer: To save uncommitted changes to a temporary storage area and restore a clean working directory

questions.question: What data structure does Git use to organize stashes?
options: A queue (FIFO — first in, first out)
A stack (LIFO — last in, first out)
questions.answer: A stack (LIFO — last in, first out)

questions.question: What is the reference name for the most recent stash?
questions.answer: stash@{0}

questions.question: Which command saves uncommitted changes with a descriptive message?
questions.answer: git stash push -m "message"

questions.question: What is the difference between `git stash pop` and `git stash apply`?
options: `pop` applies the stash and removes it from the list; `apply` applies it but keeps it in the list
`apply` applies the stash and removes it from the list; `pop` applies it but keeps it in the list
There is no difference; they are aliases for the same command
`pop` applies only tracked files; `apply` applies all files
questions.answer: `pop` applies the stash and removes it from the list; `apply` applies it but keeps it in the list

questions.question: Which flag is needed to include untracked files in a stash?
questions.answer: --include-untracked or -u

questions.question: What does `git stash list` do?
options: Lists all commits in the repository
Lists all branches that have stashes
Shows all stashes with their messages
Lists all untracked files in the working directory
questions.answer: Shows all stashes with their messages

questions.question: What does `git stash show` display?
options: The full diff of the most recent stash
A summary of the stash including files changed, insertions, and deletions
The commit message of the stash
The date and time the stash was created
questions.answer: A summary of the stash including files changed, insertions, and deletions

questions.question: What does the `-p` flag add to `git stash show`?
options: It shows the stash in a paged format
It shows the full diff of the stash
It prints only the file names
It shows the stash in patch-compatible format without diffs
questions.answer: It shows the full diff of the stash

questions.question: What happens to the stash list after running `git stash pop` successfully?
options: The applied stash remains in the list
All stashes are cleared from the list
The applied stash is removed from the list
The applied stash is moved to the bottom of the list
questions.answer: The applied stash is removed from the list

questions.question: Which command deletes the most recent stash without applying it?
questions.answer: git stash drop

questions.question: Which command deletes ALL stashes at once?
questions.answer: git stash clear

questions.question: If you have three stashes and want to apply the second oldest one, which reference do you use?
questions.answer: stash@{1}

questions.question: What is the most common real-world reason to use `git stash`?
options: To switch branches when you have uncommitted changes that aren't ready to commit
To share your work with other team members
questions.answer: To switch branches when you have uncommitted changes that aren't ready to commit

questions.question: Without the `--include-untracked` flag, what does `git stash` save?
options: Only files in the current directory
questions.answer: Only tracked modified files

questions.question: After stashing your work, what should `git status` show?
options: All your modified files as staged
questions.answer: A clean working directory

questions.question: Why might you prefer `git stash apply` over `git stash pop`?
options: `apply` keeps the stash in the list so you can apply it to multiple branches
questions.answer: `apply` keeps the stash in the list so you can apply it to multiple branches

questions.question: You create stashes in this order: Feature A, Feature B, Feature C. What is `stash@{0}`?
options: The oldest stash is always stash@{0}
questions.answer: Feature C changes

questions.question: What command applies a specific stash by its reference without removing it?
questions.answer: git stash apply stash@{1}

questions.question: In the branch-switching workflow, what is the correct sequence of steps?
options: Switch branch, stash changes, fix bug, switch back, pop stash
Stash changes, switch branch, fix bug, switch back, pop stash
Commit changes, switch branch, fix bug, switch back, revert commit
Delete changes, switch branch, fix bug, switch back, rewrite changes
questions.answer: Stash changes, switch branch, fix bug, switch back, pop stash

questions.question: What happens if you try to switch branches with uncommitted changes that conflict with the target branch?
options: Git merges the changes into the target branch
Git prevents you from switching branches
questions.answer: Git prevents you from switching branches

questions.question: After running `git stash drop`, what happens to the remaining stashes?
options: They keep their original reference numbers
They are renumbered so the next oldest becomes stash@{0}
They are moved to a backup list
questions.answer: They are renumbered so the next oldest becomes stash@{0}

questions.question: Which command is equivalent to `git stash push --include-untracked`?
questions.answer: git stash push -u

questions.question: What does `git stash` do to your working directory after saving changes?
options: It leaves the working directory unchanged
It deletes all files in the directory
It restores the working directory to the state of the last commit
It switches to the main branch automatically
questions.answer: It restores the working directory to the state of the last commit

questions.question: If `git stash list` returns empty output after a `git stash pop`, what does that confirm?
options: The stash was applied but the changes were lost
The stash was applied and successfully removed from the stash list
There is an error in the stash storage
questions.answer: The stash was applied and successfully removed from the stash list
